display.purpose: Hero headline and major identity moments.

headline.purpose: Section titles for stress results and benchmark proof.

title.purpose: Card titles, topology labels, and report summaries.

label.purpose: Metric labels, stack tags, and compact metadata.

shadows.name: interaction-glow
shadows.purpose: Subtle hover depth for clickable report cards and metric cards.

shadows.name: blue-proof-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary CTA or proof hover glow.

motion.name: reveal-standard
motion.purpose: Optional page entrance reveal, disabled under reduced motion.

components.name: Primary Documentation Button
components.description: Main documentation CTA in Python Blue.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-block; background: #4B8BBE; color: #0F0F23; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #79C0E0; color: #0F0F23; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 5px 15px rgba(75, 139, 190, 0.35); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #FFD43B; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Secondary GitHub Button
components.description: Secondary CTA in Python Yellow for repository or source paths.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-block; background: #FFD43B; color: #0F0F23; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; text-decoration: none; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #FFE873; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 5px 15px rgba(255, 212, 59, 0.35); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #79C0E0; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Stack Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 5px 13px; background: rgba(26, 42, 74, 0.75); border: 1px solid rgba(255, 212, 59, 0.5); color: #E8F4FD; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Proof Card
components.css: .ds-proof-card { display: grid; gap: 8px; padding: 24px; background: #13132E; border: 1px solid rgba(75, 139, 190, 0.18); border-radius: 12px; color: #A8CCE0; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-proof-card strong { color: #79C0E0; font-size: 35px; line-height: 1; } .ds-proof-card span { color: #E8F4FD; font-weight: 700; } .ds-proof-card small { color: #89B8D4; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Code Panel
components.description: Homepage code block for real constraints or source concepts.
components.html:
class ChallengeConstraints:
    # Resource Limits
    MAX_CPU = 1.5
    MAX_RAM_MB = 550
components.css: .ds-code-panel { margin: 0; padding: 32px; background: #050713; border: 1px solid #203754; border-radius: 8px; color: #79C0E0; overflow-x: auto; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; line-height: 1.6; } .ds-keyword { color: #FFD43B; } .ds-string { color: #A8CCE0; } .ds-comment { color: #89B8D4; font-style: italic; }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Minimal sticky project navigation with Python syntax identity.
components.html:
def rinha2_python()
GitHubDocs
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 24px; padding: 24px 32px; background: rgba(15, 15, 35, 0.85); color: #E8F4FD; font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-logo { color: #FFD43B; font-weight: 700; } .ds-logo span { color: #4B8BBE; font-weight: 400; margin-right: 8px; } .ds-links { display: flex; gap: 32px; } .ds-links a { color: #89B8D4; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; } .ds-links a:hover { color: #FFD43B; } .ds-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #FFD43B; outline-offset: 4px; }

narrative.overview: This visual system is a dark, code-native performance lab for a Python backend under contest constraints. It should feel like a measured engineering artifact: precise, compact, source-backed, and visibly under pressure.
keyCharacteristics: Dark Python night surface with controlled blue/yellow syntax accents.
Mono-forward typography used as identity, not as a blanket excuse for low readability.
Compact proof modules that connect metrics to resource limits, k6 runs, CI, and reports.
Flat technical surfaces with thin borders, tonal layering, and rare hover elevation.
Developer-native copy that names the actual stack and avoids generic superlatives.

rules.name: The Proof Color Rule
rules.body: Blue means interaction or evidence. Yellow means syntax or exceptional emphasis. If a color does not clarify either, remove it.

rules.name: The No Neon Rule
rules.body: This is Python under pressure, not cyberpunk. Avoid purple glows, neon gradients, and glassy overlays.

rules.name: The Monospace Earns Its Place Rule
rules.body: Monospace is identity when it marks code, metrics, stack, and proof. It becomes friction when it turns every paragraph into a terminal wall.

rules.name: The Specificity Rule
rules.body: Replace generic hype with stack, constraint, run, and report details.

rules.name: The Flat Evidence Rule
rules.body: Evidence modules sit on the surface. They do not need glass, blur, or heavy shadow to be credible.

rules.name: The Motion Opt-Out Rule
rules.body: Every particle, ambient drift, entrance reveal, and hover transform must respect prefers-reduced-motion.

dos: Do preserve the dark Python/code aesthetic: Deep Python Night, Python Blue, Python Yellow, and the def rinha2_python() identity.
Do connect metrics to k6, CI, report date, resource envelope, or commit context.
Do name the actual implementation stack: Flask, Gunicorn, psycopg2, NGINX, PostgreSQL procedures.
Do keep proof above the fold when possible: constraints, latest run, and report access should be visible early.
Do add visible :focus-visible states and reduced-motion handling before shipping visual polish.
Do use semantic headings. Section titles should be H2; card titles should be H3.
donts: Don't describe the implementation as Asyncio or Python Async unless the source code actually uses that architecture.
Don't mix Gatling and k6 terminology. Pick the verified benchmark tool and use it consistently.
Don't use generic SaaS landing-page copy such as incredibly powerful without proof.
Don't use glassmorphism, purple gradients, neon cyberpunk lighting, or AI-tool marketing aesthetics.
Don't use side-stripe borders greater than 1px as card accents. Use full borders, syntax labels, or metric structure instead.
Don't present fake benchmark dashboards, unsupported metrics, or simulated terminal output as fact.
Don't let decorative particles, clipped circles, or motion compete with metrics and reports.
